OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):

I spend a few times a week scrolling through my feeds, and it's getting exhausting. I see posts that are completely fabricated, spreading misinformation and anger. It's like a never-ending cycle of negativity. I've tried to limit my social media use, but it's hard when everyone around me is talking about the latest viral sensations. I've even seen friends sharing fake news without realizing it. It's like they're drinking the Kool-Aid. I've started to question everything I read online, even my favorite news outlets. It's a scary thought, but what if everything I trust is wrong? I've always been a skeptical person, but this has taken it to a whole new level. I've started to rely more on word of mouth and good old-fashioned research. It's slower, but at least I know I'm getting the truth. response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

persona.time_onsocialmedia: You spend roughly a few times weekly on social media

response_parsed.opinion: As a delivery driver in Bologna, I've noticed the rise of fake content on social media, and it's affecting my mood. I spend a few times a week scrolling through my feeds, and it's getting exhausting. I see posts that are completely fabricated, spreading misinformation and anger. It's like a never-ending cycle of negativity. I've tried to limit my social media use, but it's hard when everyone around me is talking about the latest viral sensations. I've even seen friends sharing fake news without realizing it. It's like they're drinking the Kool-Aid. I've started to question everything I read online, even my favorite news outlets. It's a scary thought, but what if everything I trust is wrong? I've always been a skeptical person, but this has taken it to a whole new level. I've started to rely more on word of mouth and good old-fashioned research. It's slower, but at least I know I'm getting the truth. I've also tried to engage more with my friends and family, putting down my phone and talking face-to-face. It's amazing how much more connected I feel when I'm not staring at a screen. I wish more people would do the same. We need to take back control of our online experiences and demand better from ourselves and others. It's time to call out fake news and misinformation for what it is – a threat to our mental and emotional well-being. We owe it to ourselves and our communities to be more discerning and critical in our online interactions. It's a challenge, but I'm up for it. I just hope others will join me.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: I deduced that fake content on social media was affecting my mood, so I took steps to limit my exposure and rely more on trusted sources. I also tried to engage more with friends and family, putting down my phone and talking face-to-face. I realized that our online experiences have a direct impact on our mental and emotional well-being, and we need to take responsibility for what we consume and share online. I believe that by being more discerning and critical, we can create a more positive and trustworthy online environment.
